intro:
I couldn't find any information on the plant Sclarea dumetorum. However, I found Sclarea (also known as Clary Sage) which seems to be a closer match. Here's a paragraph about it:

Sclarea (Sclarea notatum or Salvia sclarea) is a flowering plant in the mint family, known for its distinctive, sweet, and herbaceous fragrance. The health benefits of Sclarea include its ability to reduce stress, anxiety, and insomnia due to its high content of linalool and linalyl acetate, which also have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Sclarea include volatile oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, with linalool and linalyl acetate being the most significant components. Sclarea is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, essential oils, and tinctures, and is commonly used in aromatherapy, skincare products, and herbal remedies for stress relief and menstrual issues.
